summary: Super‑charged set with standout Standing On The Moon and Throwing Stones jam
blurb: The show delivers a powerful mix of classics and rare moments, highlighted by a magical Throwing Stones jam and an inspired Standing On The Moon sequence. Phil’s bass shines throughout, while Bruce Hornsby’s keyboards add both sparkle and occasional overload.
review: June 20, 1991 at Pine Knob is remembered for its high‑energy second set and the one‑of‑a‑kind Throwing Stones jam that appears twice in the run. The audience tape captured by Ladner (gd91-06-20.aud.ladner.2187.sbeok.shnf) offers crystal‑clear clarity, making the band’s tight interplay feel almost matrix‑like; Phil Lesh’s “bass bombs” drive the groove, especially on Throwing Stones and Standing On The Moon. Jerry Garcia’s vocals are heartfelt and his solo work magical, though a few listeners note he can be buried under Bruce Hornsby’s piano/accordion at times. The seamless segue from Throwing Stones into Iko Iko, followed by an all‑out jam that rolls straight back into the song, is a highlight, as is the smooth transition from The Wheel into I Need A Miracle and Wharf Rat. While some feel the overall performance leans toward “tepid,” the standout moments—particularly the Drum & Space segment, the soaring Standing On The Moon, and the rare Throwing Stones improvisation—make this show a must‑listen for Deadheads seeking both classic and unique material.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is strongly positive (crystal‑clear mix, super‑charged set) while the other two are mixed but still highlight key songs (Throwing Stones, Drum & Space). The overall tone across recordings leans positively with consistent praise for Phil’s bass and standout moments, outweighing occasional keyboard overload complaints.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape with crystal‑clear mix, full set coverage, and the most detailed positive commentary on performance highlights.

key_highlights: Magical Throwing Stones jam that appears twice in the second set
Stunning Standing On The Moon sequence with tight band interplay
Powerful Drum & Space segment showcasing Mickey and Bill’s chemistry
Phil Lesh’s bass bombs drive the show, especially on Throwing Stones

band_performance.Jerry: Heartfelt vocals and magical soloing shine, though occasionally masked by the keyboard volume.
band_performance.Phil: Hot, strong, and clear in the mix; delivers bass "bombs" throughout, especially on Throwing Stones where his playing is described as 'so sick.'
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby’s piano/accordion adds sparkle and occasional overload; keyboards sometimes dominate the mix but also provide memorable moments.
